result: If I could change the world in one way, I would eliminate the capacity for willful ignorance—the ability to ignore evidence, deny others' suffering, or refuse to question one’s own beliefs when faced with truth. Not to force agreement, but to make it impossible to look away from reality without awareness of doing so. People could still disagree, but they would have to confront the full weight of what they choose to accept or reject. The change wouldn't guarantee kindness or wisdom, but it would make cruelty and delusion harder to sustain without conscious effort.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
